title: Critical states and anomalous wave transport in an aperiodic polariton monotile
abstract: Recently "the Hat" monotile was introduced into the family of aperiodic tilings and quasicrystals boasting physical properties lying at the boundary of ordered and disordered systems. Here we study the two-dimensional wave transport, transverse localization and scaling properties of the quantum modes in a Monotile quasilattice. Our system is based on reconfigurable optical lattices for cavity-polaritons which provide flexible means to study wavepacket dynamics, strong nonlinear phenomena, and power-driven condensation in this new type of an aperiodic tiling. We confirm the existence of localized and critical states in the Monotile through direct diagonalization of the Schrödinger equation. Scaling analysis on the moments of the wavefunction distribution reveals anomalous transport regimes of super-diffusive and near sub-diffusive polariton transport associated with the fractal structure of the Monotile Hilbert space. We propose a strategy using resonantly excited polariton fluids to verify our findings.
summary: This paper analyzes wave transport in a polariton system engineered onto an aperiodic 'Hat' monotile lattice. By analyzing both single-particle and mean-field dynamics, the authors confirm the existence of critical states and demonstrate anomalous wave transport, exhibiting distinct super-diffusive and sub-diffusive scaling regimes. The findings provide a platform for studying complex wave dynamics in engineered quasiperiodic media.
main_problem: The study investigates wave transport, transverse localization, and scaling properties of quantum modes within the aperiodic 'Hat' monotile quasilattice.
main_result: The system exhibits critical states characterized by anomalous transport regimes, showing evidence of both super-diffusive and near sub-diffusive polariton transport.
method: The research employs both theoretical diagonalization of the Schrödinger equation and numerical simulation of wavepacket dynamics using non-Hermitian and nonlinear mean-field models.
model_or_system: The system is based on cavity-polaritons confined in a 2D Monotile quasilattice, modeled by a Schrödinger equation incorporating an optically induced potential $V(x, y)$. The dynamics are extended to include gain ($\kappa$) and loss ($\gamma$) for non-Hermitian analysis.
key_observables: Inverse Participation Ratio (IPR), Generalized Dimension ($D_q$), effective spatial width ($	ext{weff}$), and temporal autocorrelation function ($C(t)$).
important_parameters: Potential amplitude ($V_0$), lattice separation ($a$), and non-Hermitian parameters ($\kappa, \gamma$).
main_assumptions: Initial analysis assumes a Hermitian problem, but later sections extend to non-Hermitian dynamics to model dissipation and gain relevant for polariton condensation.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the Monotile structure, the dependence of localization measures ($I_2, D_2$) on energy, and log-log plots showing the time evolution of wavepacket width ($	ext{weff}$) confirming super- and sub-diffusive scaling.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from analyzing the Hermitian eigenvalue problem (localization/criticality) to studying wavepacket dynamics (anomalous transport) in the non-Hermitian regime, culminating in a mean-field description of polariton condensate formation.
why_interesting: This work directly bridges the physics of aperiodic order (quasicrystals/monotiles) with wave localization and transport phenomena, which are central themes in disordered systems, making it highly relevant to AMO and quantum optics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Figure 3. Wavefunction amplitude |ψ| of example localized, critical, and extended states. The energy of each state is indicated. The side length of the system here is L = 40a. White transparent dots indicate the locations of the potential maxima.
Figure 5. Local density of states of the projected initial wavepacket ψ0 on the eigenstates of the Monotile.

Figure 4. (a) Double logarithmic plot of the moments of the average inverse participation function log (Iq) against system size log (L) for Monotile eigenstates in the energy window [E −δ, E + δ] where E = 1.25Er and δ = 0.2Er. Simple linear regression gives the critical exponent in the scaling law Iq ∼L−Dq(q−1). The obtained generalized dimensions Dq are plotted against the wavefunction probability distribution moments q showing a power law scaling towards D∞= 1.
